questions.question: Direction: Choose the word which is most nearly the antonym in the meaning as the word printed in Bold. Untenable
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Sustainable'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Untenable means 'not able to be maintained or defended (बनाए रखने या बचाव करने में अक्षम)'. • ​Ex: There has always been untenable in his life so they could not save his marriage. • Marked option '​Sustainable' means 'able to continue for a long time (लंबे समय तक जारी रखने में सक्षम, टिकाऊ)'. • Ex: The company's aim is to deliver high quality, sustainable growth in India. • It is clear that Untenable and Sustainable are opposite in meaning. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Changeable: that can be unstable or exchangeable (जो अस्थिर या विनिमय योग्य हो, अस्थिर). • Shaky: something that is unstable or weak (कुछ ऐसा जो अस्थिर या कमजोर हो,अस्थिर). • Unstable: that which is not stationary (जो स्थिर न हो).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Camaraderie
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Dislike'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The most appropriate antonym of the given word 'Camaraderie' is 'Dislike'. • Camaraderie: a feeling of friendship and trust among a group of people who work together or share an experience. [(साथ काम करनेवाले लोगों के बीच) परस्पर मित्रता एवं विश्‍वास की भावना; सौहार्द, पारस्परिक विश्‍वास] • Example: A spirit of camaraderie is not always seen among people from the same fraternity. • Dislike: to think that somebody/something is unpleasant. [(किसी व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु को) नापसंद करना] • Example: I really dislike flying. Correct Answer: Dislike. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Let's look at the meaning of other words: • Friendship: a relationship between people who are friends. (मित्रता; दोस्‍ती) • Amity: friendly relations. (मैत्रीपूर्ण संबंध) • Companionship: the pleasant feeling of having a friendly relationship with somebody and not being alone. (साहचर्य, मैत्री, साथ)

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in the direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. Rani said to Reema, "Did you know he was not coming to my birthday?''
options: Rani asked Reema if she knew he had not gone to her birthday.
Rani asked Reema if she knew he had not been going to her birthday.
Rani asked Reema if she had known he had not been going to her birthday.
Rani asked Reema if she knew he was not going to her birthday.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is '4' i.e. Rani asked Reema if she knew he was not going to her birthday. . ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The sentence is a direct question in the **past tense** with **"Did you know"** as the reporting verb. • In indirect speech, the question is changed to a statement, and the auxiliary verb **"did"** is removed. • Since the reporting verb is in the **past tense** (**"asked"**), the verb **"was not coming"** is retained as **"was not going"** in indirect speech. • The use of **"if"** introduces the indirect question, and **"she knew"** is used to maintain the tense consistency. Correct sentence: Rani asked Reema if she knew he was not going to her birthday. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Indirect Speech: • Structure: Reporting verb + **if/whether** + subject + verb. • Example: She asked if he was coming to the party. • Usage: Used to report questions and statements from direct speech. • Question Reporting: • Structure: In questions, we remove auxiliary verbs and use **if** or **whether** for yes/no questions. • Example: He asked whether she was coming. • Usage: To report direct questions in a more formal or indirect way.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A pause between the acts of a play
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Interlude'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Let's understand the meaning of the correct option: • Interlude: a period of time between two events during which something different happens. • Example: They always met in the city for their romantic interludes. Hence, the correct answer is Option 1. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Let's explore the other given options: • Interline: insert words between the lines of (a document or other text). • Interlace: to join things together by twisting them and crossing them over each other. • Interlink: join or connect(two or more things) together. Thus, from the above meanings correct one-word substitution for the given question is 'Interlude'.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The ancient banyan tree is worshipped by the villagers.
options: The villagers worshipped the ancient banyan tree.
The villagers are worshipping the ancient banyan tree.
The villagers worship the ancient banyan tree.
The villagers have worshipped the ancient banyan tree.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'The villagers worship the ancient banyan tree.' ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is in passive voice. As per the question we have to change it into active voice. • The structure of the given sentence is as follows: • Subject (objective case) + to be + past participle + Object (subjective case) (Passive voice) • Subject + infinitive + Object (Active voice) • Example: • Fresh vegetables are sold by the grocer. (Passive voice) • The grocer sells fresh vegetables. (Active voice) • The subject (objective case) of the given sentence is ‘the ancient banyan tree’. • The object (subjective case) of the given sentence is ‘the villagers’. • The subject will be put in place of the object and the object will be put in place of the subject. • is worshipped will be changed to worship. Correct sentence - The villagers worship the ancient banyan tree.

questions.question: In the following questions, some parts of a sentence have been jumbled up. Rearrange these parts which are labelled as P, Q, R, S to produce the correct sentence. Summer vacation P: that their children learn something worthwhile Q: who are able to provide services for anxious parents R : who want to make sure S: has become a big business for those
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'SQRP'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points While arranging the parts of the sentence, we have to find some grammatical or contextual relationships between them. So let's find them out. • The given noun subject 'Summer vacation' needs a verb to move forward. The only alternative with that is Part S. Reading makes sense that 'Summer vacation has become a big business...' Hence, it will be put in the first place. • Next, we can see that the previous part ended with 'for those' which will need a relative/ adjective clause that would define or describe 'who.' Between Part Q and R, we can see that 'who are able to provide services for anxious parents' fits into the space for meaning. Further moving forward along the sentence would clarify and justify the choice. So, Part Q will take second place. • After that would come Part R, which gives more information about the kind of the parents it is talking about. So, R will take third place. • Part P would come at the end. 'that' as a conjunction clarifies what the parents want to make sure (that their children learn something worthwhile). • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct order is 'SQRP'. Hence, the sentence in the correct order is 'Summer vacation has become a big business for those who are able to provide services for anxious parents who want to make sure that their children learn something worthwhile.'

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Usually, Ratheesh hasn’t/ take part in these types / of events because of / his hectic schedule.
questions.explanation: The erroneous part is 'Usually, Ratheesh hasn't'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect. • Here, 'Usually, Ratheesh doesn't' should be used instead of 'Usually, Ratheesh hasn't'. • The word "hasn't" is a contraction of "has not," which is present perfect tense. • However, in the sentence, we need to use the simple present tense because the sentence is talking about Ratheesh's usual behavior. The verb "doesn't" is the contracted form of "does not," which is the correct verb form to use in this sentence. • Thus, the correct answer is Option 4. Correct Sentence: Usually, Ratheesh doesn't take part in these types of events because of his hectic schedule.

questions.question: Consider the statement and fill in the blank. I won't lend you my car____ you promise to drive carefully.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'unless'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Let's explore options: • unless: It is a conjunction and is used to introduce the case in which a statement being made is not true or valid. • Example: Unless you have a photographic memory, repetition is vital. • except: It is used as preposition and function used to introduce something which is not including; other than. • Example: They work every day except Sunday. • in case: as a provision against something happening or being true. • Example: We put on thick jumpers, in case it was cold • unlike: it is used to express something different from; not similar to. • Example: She's unlike the rest of her family. • Thus, from above we can conclude 'unless' will fit the blank. Therefore, the correct sentence is: 'I won't lend you my car unless you promise to drive carefully.'

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. India is celebrating the 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava this year.
options: The 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava has been celebrated by India this year.
The 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava was celebrated by India this year.
This year India is being celebrated by the 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava.
The 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava is being celebrated by India this year.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "The 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava is being celebrated by India this year." ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The tense of given sentence is "Present Continuous" • ​Active Voice (Present continuous) Subject + am/is/are/ + Mainverb (ing form) + object • Passive Voice (Present Continuous) Object + am /is/are+ being + main verb (3rd form) + by + Subject. • "India" is the Subject of the Sentence. • Celebrate is the Main verb • the 75th Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsava this year is the object. • So according to the structure of the passive voice the correct answer is option no 4.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Vishnu was happy to push the boat out for his fiancée’s birthday.
options: and felt so, that his heart started beating faster on
to spend a lot of money because he was celebrating
to take risks that could cause problems or danger on
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option (3) i.e. "to spend a lot of money because he was celebrating". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • To push the boat out for: This means to spend a lot of money on something, usually for a celebration. (उत्सव के लिए बहुत सारा पैसा खर्च करना) • Example: They really pushed the boat out for their wedding anniversary. • To spend a lot of money because he was celebrating: (क्योंकि वह जश्न मना रहा था इसलिए बहुत सारा पैसा खर्च किया) - To use a large amount of money for celebration. • Example: He decided to spend a lot of money on a lavish party to celebrate his promotion. • This fits the context where Vishnu wants to celebrate his fiancée's birthday in a special way. Therefore, the correct answer is "to spend a lot of money because he was celebrating". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • But seemed like pretending on: (नाटक करने जैसा लग रहा था) - Appears as if faking or pretending. • And felt so, that his heart started beating faster on: (और ऐसा महसूस हुआ कि उसका दिल जोर से धड़कने लगा) - Felt an intense emotional or physical reaction. • To take risks that could cause problems or danger on: (जोखिम उठाना जिससे समस्याएँ या खतरा हो सकता है) - Engaging in actions that could potentially lead to problems or danger.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Slacken
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option 2 i.e. Decrease. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "slacken" means to make or become less active, vigorous, or intense; to loosen or relax. (ढीला करना) • Example: During the holidays, many employees tend to slacken their work pace. • "Decrease" refers to making something smaller or less in size, amount, intensity, or degree. (घटाना) • Example: The company decided to decrease its production due to low demand. • Hence, we can infer that "slacken" is synonymous with "decrease". Therefore, the correct answer is: Option 2 i.e. Decrease. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences: • Irritate (चिढ़ाना): To make someone annoyed or a little angry. • Example: Loud noises can irritate people who are trying to concentrate. • Tighten (कसना): To make or become tight or tighter. • Example: Make sure to tighten the screws to secure the shelf properly. • Increase (बढ़ाना): To make or become greater in size, amount, or degree. • Example: The company plans to increase its workforce by 20% next year.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in the direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. Sita said, "Wow! what a beautiful house it is".
options: Sita said that it is a beautiful house.
Sita exclaimed with wonder what a beautiful house it is.
Sita exclaimed with wonder that it was a beautiful house.
Sita told it was a beautiful house.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option 3 i.e. Sita exclaimed with wonder that it was a beautiful house. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is in direct speech and expresses an exclamatory feeling (Wow!). • The word “exclaimed with wonder” is used in indirect speech to convey surprise or admiration. (आश्चर्यचकित होकर exclaimed करना) • In indirect speech, the present tense (“is”) generally changes to past tense (“was”). (is → was) • Therefore, the indirect form is: Sita exclaimed with wonder that it was a beautiful house. (सीता ने आश्चर्यचकित होकर कहा कि वह बहुत सुंदर घर था।) • Example: Direct: He said, "Alas! I am ruined." → Indirect: He exclaimed with sorrow that he was ruined. Therefore, the correct answer is: Sita exclaimed with wonder that it was a beautiful house. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences: • Option 1: Sita said that it is a beautiful house. (सीता ने कहा कि यह एक सुंदर घर है।) • This option simply states a fact and misses the exclamatory emotion and the required change of tense. • Option 2: Sita exclaimed with wonder what a beautiful house it is. (सीता ने आश्चर्यचकित होकर कहा कि यह कितना सुंदर घर है।) • This option does not convert the tense and structure as required in indirect speech. • Option 4: Sita told it was a beautiful house. (सीता ने कहा कि यह एक सुंदर घर था।) • "Told" is not the correct reporting verb for exclamatory sentences, and it misses expressing wonder.

questions.question: Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Glovve". ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Among the given options, the word in option 2 is incorrectly spelled. • The word "Glovve" is incorrectly spelled as it contains an extra "v". • The correct spelling of the word is "Glove". • "Glove" refers to a covering for the hand, usually made of cloth or leather.(दस्ताना) • Example: He wore a pair of gloves to protect his hands from the cold. Hence, the correct answer is option 2. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Option 1, "Wider", is spelled correctly and means having a greater width.(व्यापक) • Option 3, "Hide", is spelled correctly and means to conceal or keep out of sight.(छिपाना) • Option 4, "Fold", is spelled correctly and means to bend or crease something.(तह करना)

questions.question: Improve the underlined part of the sentence. Choose 'No improvement' as an answer if the sentence is grammatically correct. An ant can carry up to 50 times it's body weight.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The primary issue in the sentence is the incorrect use of "it's" instead of "its". • "It's" is a contraction for "it is" or "it has," whereas "its" is a possessive pronoun. • The correct phrase should be "times its," ensuring grammatical correctness and clarity. • Therefore, the correct answer is Option 2. Correct Sentence: "An ant can carry up to 50 times its body weight." ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • No improvement – This would be the correct option if the sentence were already grammatically correct, but it is not.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. It turned out to be a wild-goose chase for the police.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fruitless pursuit or search ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The idiom "wild-goose chase" means a fruitless pursuit or a pointless search, typically involving a lot of effort or energy without any likelihood of success. (एक निरर्थक प्रयास या निरर्थक खोज, जिसमें आम तौर पर सफलता की किसी भी संभावना के बिना बहुत अधिक प्रयास या ऊर्जा शामिल होती है) • Example: The detectives went on a wild-goose chase trying to track down the suspect based on unreliable witness testimony. Therefore, the correct answer is option 3.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. He used to reply / that the only place / for which he went / was the town library.
questions.explanation: The erroneous part of the sentence is: for which he went ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Replace the preposition "for" with "to" to correct the sentence. • For is used to talk about a purpose or a reason for something. • Example - I'm going for some breakfast. • To is used to indicate the direction or destination of a particular place or something. • Example - We're going to Liverpool next week. • In the given sentence "to" should be used because a destination place is mentioned, that is the "town library." • 'Which' is used after a preposition to represent a specified antecedent. • The implied meaning of the sentence is: He used to respond by saying that the local library was the only location he visited. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2, which contains an error. Corrected sentence: He used to reply that the only place to which he went was the town library.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. The use of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides began after the Green Revolution.
options: A practice of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides
The custom of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides
Consumption of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides
The usage of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'The usage of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The given sentence is: "The use of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides began after the Green Revolution." • The underlined part of the sentence is "The use of." While grammatically correct, it lacks variety and could be improved for better readability and style. Let's analyze each of the options: • A practice of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides: This option suggests that "chemicals, insecticides, and pesticides" themselves are being practiced, which doesn't convey the intended meaning. Also, "practice" usually refers to repeated actions, while "use" denotes the action of employing something for a specific purpose. • The custom of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides: "Custom" typically refers to a traditional or habitual way of behaving or doing something within a particular society, which is not suitable in this context. • Consumption of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides: "Consumption" usually refers to the act of using up a resource, typically in large quantities, often implying ingestion or depletion. However, in this context, the focus is on the application or utilization of chemicals, insecticides, and pesticides rather than their consumption. • The usage of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides: "Usage" refers to the action of using something, and it fits well in the context of the sentence. It maintains clarity and conciseness while offering a varied alternative to "use." • Therefore, option 4) "The usage of chemicals, insecticides and pesticides" is the most appropriate choice as it effectively conveys the intended meaning and improves the style of the sentence by providing a synonym for "use" without introducing ambiguity or altering the context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. SMUDGE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'stain'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • Smudge is a noun which means a blurred or smeared mark on the surface of something (किसी चीज की सतह पर धुंधला या धुंधला निशान।). • Example - There was a faint smudge of scarlet below the pressure lock. • The meaning of the given options are as follows - • stain - a colored patch or dirty mark that is difficult to remove. (एक रंगीन पैच या गंदा निशान जिसे हटाना मुश्किल है।) • sweet - having the pleasant taste characteristic of sugar or honey; not salt, sour, or bitter. (चीनी या शहद के सुखद स्वाद की विशेषता होना; नमक, खट्टा या कड़वा नहीं।) • trash - waste material; refuse. (कचरा) • mud - soft, sticky matter resulting from the mixing of earth and water. (कीचड़) • From the above meanings it is evident that stain is the correct synonym for the word smudge.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate choice. The problem is, I've got more ________ friends than available friends.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is grounded. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key PointsLet's understand the meaning of the marked word: • Grounded: well balanced and sensible(अच्छी तरह से संतुलित और समझदार) • Example: For someone so young, Chris is extremely grounded. The sentence conveys the meaning: I have more sensible friends than those who are available. Thus, grounded is the apt. word for the blank. Correct sentence: The problem is, I've got more grounded friends than available friends. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Lunatic: a person who is mentally ill (not in technical use)(एक व्यक्ति जो मानसिक रूप से बीमार है (तकनीकी उपयोग में नहीं) • Ferocious: savagely fierce, cruel, or violent(बेरहमी से भयंकर, क्रूर, या हिंसक) • Rounded: having a smooth, curved surface(एक चिकनी, घुमावदार सतह होना)

questions.question: In the following questions, some parts of a sentence have been jumbled up. Rearrange these parts which are labelled as P, Q, R, S to produce the correct sentence. Each culture P: flourishes when it comes Q: own tradition and yet R: draws strength from its S: into contact with others
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'RQPS'. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points While arranging the parts of the sentence, we have to find some grammatical or contextual relationships between them. So let's find them out. • As the Subject 'Each culture' has been given, it is most likely that a verb will follow it. In the following case two parts (P and R) haves verb at their beginning. • Putting Part P at the beginning would leave out at least two parts unresolved. Instead, Part R would come first as it means 'Each culture draws strength from its...' • Part Q comes next. It mentions where each culture draws strength from (own tradition) and ends with a conjunction 'yet' which indicates that there will probably be some contrary fact or information next. • Part P follows Part Q: it describes what happens (flourishes when it comes...) • Part S comes at the end. We can see that 'comes into contact' is a commonly used phrase and can be used as hint to reassemble the original sentence. • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct order is 'RQPS'. Hence, the sentence in the correct order is 'Each culture draws strength from its own tradition and yet flourishes when it comes into contact with others.'

questions.question: Read the passage carefully and select the correct answer for the given blanks out of the four alternatives. Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology (1) _________ the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and (2) _________. The history of psychology also provides perspective. Rather than a dry collection of names and dates, the history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that (3) _________ who we are. Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a (4) _________ of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" The importance of history in defining (5) _________ we are cannot be overstated. Select the most appropriate option for blank(1)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "offers" means to present or proffer something for someone to accept or reject as desired. (प्रस्तावित करना) • The phrase "the history of psychology (1) offers the opportunity" makes logical sense as it suggests that history provides a chance to learn or understand something. • The other options: "pieces," "makes," and "talks" do not fit the context as appropriately. Complete Sentence: Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology offers the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and developed. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Pieces (टुकड़े): means parts of a thing, not suitable for the context here as it doesn't align with "the history of psychology" • Makes (बनाना): means to create or form, less natural in this context • Talks (बातें करना): means to speak, not fitting in the given sentence structure

questions.question: Read the passage carefully and select the correct answer for the given blanks out of the four alternatives. Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology (1) _________ the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and (2) _________. The history of psychology also provides perspective. Rather than a dry collection of names and dates, the history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that (3) _________ who we are. Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a (4) _________ of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" The importance of history in defining (5) _________ we are cannot be overstated. Select the most appropriate option for blank (2)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "developed" means to grow or cause to grow and become more mature, advanced, or elaborate. (विकसित) • The phrase "how it has grown and developed" is logical and suggests a process of growth and further advancement over time. • The other options: "solved," "juggled," and "erupted" do not fit the context or convey the intended meaning. Complete Sentence: Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology offers the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and developed. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Solved (हल किया): means finding an answer to a problem, not suitable in context. • Juggled (जुगलिंग करना): means continuously toss in the air and catch (a number of objects), not fitting in this context. • Erupted (फूटना): means to explode, also not suitable for the context.

questions.question: Read the passage carefully and select the correct answer for the given blanks out of the four alternatives. Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology (1) _________ the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and (2) _________. The history of psychology also provides perspective. Rather than a dry collection of names and dates, the history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that (3) _________ who we are. Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a (4) _________ of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" The importance of history in defining (5) _________ we are cannot be overstated. Select the most appropriate option for blank (3)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "defines" means to describe the nature or essential qualities of something. (परिभाषित करता है) • The phrase "the important intersection of time and place that defines who we are" makes logical sense as it suggests that these factors shape or determine our identity. • The other options: "mends," "conceals," and "transports" do not fit the context or convey the intended meaning. Complete Sentence: The history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that defines who we are. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Mends (मरम्मत करना): means to repair or fix, not suitable in context. • Conceals (छिपाना): means to hide or keep something secret, also not suitable for the context. • Transports (लाना): means to move or carry from one place to another, also not fitting in the given sentence structure.

questions.question: Read the passage carefully and select the correct answer for the given blanks out of the four alternatives. Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology (1) _________ the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and (2) _________. The history of psychology also provides perspective. Rather than a dry collection of names and dates, the history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that (3) _________ who we are. Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a (4) _________ of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" The importance of history in defining (5) _________ we are cannot be overstated. Select the most appropriate option for blank (4)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "series" means a number of things, events, or people of a similar kind that follow one after the other. (श्रृंखला) • The phrase "the conversation usually begins with a series of questions" makes logical sense as it implies a succession of questions typically asked to get to know someone. • The other options: "pairs," "classes," and "parts" do not fit the context as appropriately. They do not convey the idea of a sequence or succession of questions. Complete Sentence: Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a series of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Pairs (जोड़े): means two things that are together, not suitable in the context of asking questions. • Classes (प्रकार): means categories or groups, not fitting in this context. • Parts (भाग): means pieces or segments of a whole, also not fitting the context of sequential questioning.

questions.question: Read the passage carefully and select the correct answer for the given blanks out of the four alternatives. Psychology is an exciting field and the history of psychology (1) _________ the opportunity to make sense of how it has grown and (2) _________. The history of psychology also provides perspective. Rather than a dry collection of names and dates, the history of psychology tells us about the important intersection of time and place that (3) _________ who we are. Consider what happens when you meet someone for the first time. The conversation usually begins with a (4) _________ of questions such as, "Where did you grow up?" "How long have you lived here?" "Where did you go to school?" The importance of history in defining (5) _________ we are cannot be overstated. Select the most appropriate option for blank (5)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/key-point-image.png) Key Points • The word "who" is used to introduce a clause giving further information about a person or people previously mentioned. (कौन) • The phrase "defining who we are" correctly refers to people and makes logical sense, as it talks about personal identity. • The other options: "whom," "that," and "which" do not fit the context as appropriately. "Whom" is used as the object of a verb or preposition, "that" is generally used to refer to things not people, and "which" is used for things or animals in a non-restrictive clause. Complete Sentence: The importance of history in defining who we are cannot be overstated. ![](https://cdn.testbook.com/resources/lms_creative_elements/additional-information-image.png) Additional Information • Whom (किसे): Used as the object of a verb or preposition, not suitable for the subject of the clause here. • That (वह): Used to introduce a clause defining a person or thing previously mentioned, more commonly for things. • Which (जो): Used for non-restrictive clauses, not appropriate for referring to people in this context.
